title: phlox longifolia var. humilis
intro: Phlox longifolia var. humilis, also known as low phlox or dwarf phlox, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Phlox longifolia var. humilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various skin issues. Herbal preparations of Phlox longifolia var. humilis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
